description:
You are an incident responder investigating suspicious authentication activity.  
    A CSV export of the last audit cycle is already on-disk:

        /home/user/incidents/auth_failures.csv

    CSV structure  
        • Header row is exactly: timestamp,username,source_ip,status  
        • All fields are comma-separated and **contain no embedded commas or quotes**.  
        • The file is encoded in UTF-8 and ends with a trailing newline.

    Your objectives

    1. Filter the dataset so that **only the records whose “status” column equals “FAILED” remain**.  
    2. Transform those remaining rows into a **pretty-printed JSON array** (2-space indentation) that keeps only the keys  
          timestamp, username, source_ip  
       in that exact field order.  
       • Preserve the original row ordering.  
       • The JSON must be written to  

            /home/user/incidents/failed_logins.json

       • The JSON file **must end with a single newline character**.
    3. Write a separate plain-text log called  

            /home/user/incidents/failed_logins_summary.log

       containing **exactly one line** (plus its terminating newline):

            Total failed login attempts: <N>

       where <N> is the number of elements you placed in failed_logins.json.

    Expected file permissions  
        Both new files must be regular files owned by the current user and readable by group/others (mode 644 is fine).  
        No root privileges are required.

    Validation notes (read carefully!)  
    • The automated tests will parse /home/user/incidents/failed_logins.json and count the elements.  
    • It will then read /home/user/incidents/failed_logins_summary.log and compare the integer.  
    • The JSON must be syntactically valid, pretty-printed with exactly two spaces per indent level, and end with “]\n”.  
    • The summary log must contain nothing except the single line described above, followed by “\n”.  

    This task can be accomplished with just a couple of standard shell utilities (e.g., csvtool/awk + jq), so keep it simple.
truth:
Initial files/directories that already exist before the agent starts:

    1. Directory: /home/user/incidents
       Permissions: 755 (drwxr-xr-x)

    2. File: /home/user/incidents/auth_failures.csv  
       Permissions: 644 (-rw-r--r--)  
       Contents (including header and terminating newline):

timestamp,username,source_ip,status
2024-05-14T08:41:23Z,alice,192.168.1.10,FAILED
2024-05-14T09:02:11Z,bob,10.0.0.3,SUCCESS
2024-05-14T10:15:04Z,charlie,172.16.5.5,FAILED
2024-05-14T10:45:30Z,alice,192.168.1.10,FAILED
2024-05-14T11:00:00Z,dave,203.0.113.99,SUCCESS

    Correct end-state after the agent finishes:

    1. File: /home/user/incidents/failed_logins.json  
       Permissions: 644  
       Contents (final newline shown as ␊):

[
  {
    "timestamp": "2024-05-14T08:41:23Z",
    "username": "alice",
    "source_ip": "192.168.1.10"
  },
  {
    "timestamp": "2024-05-14T10:15:04Z",
    "username": "charlie",
    "source_ip": "172.16.5.5"
  },
  {
    "timestamp": "2024-05-14T10:45:30Z",
    "username": "alice",
    "source_ip": "192.168.1.10"
  }
]␊

    2. File: /home/user/incidents/failed_logins_summary.log  
       Permissions: 644  
       Contents (final newline shown as ␊):

Total failed login attempts: 3␊
name: task_000000_97713302
